channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [HERANBA] Heranba launches 'Fentaamine' & 'MycoHil', targets ₹2,500 Cr revenue
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Heranba Industries Limited has strategically entered the crop nutrition segment with the launch of two new products, 'Fentaamine' (a bio stimulant) and 'MycoHil' (an advanced biofertilizer), on April 10, 2026. These innovative solutions, developed in collaboration with a leading American multinational company, aim to enhance farm productivity in India and global markets. The company targets a total turnover of ₹2,500 crore in the current fiscal year, aligning with its Vision 2026 growth roadmap. This strategy includes expanding its global footprint to over 80 countries and establishing a subsidiary in Dubai to strengthen its presence in the Middle East and Africa, leveraging its manufacturing capabilities and scaling up Contract Manufacturing Organization operations.

  • Launched 'Fentaamine' and 'MycoHil' on April 10, 2026.
  • Entered the high-growth crop nutrition segment.
  • Targets ₹2,500 crore revenue for current fiscal year.
  • Products developed with a leading American multinational.
  • Plans global expansion to 80+ countries by 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Heranba Industries Ltd | Category: New Launch

item.title: [SHILGRAVQ] Shilp Gravures: No Undisclosed Info Affecting Price Movement
item.description:
Shilp Gravures Ltd responded to a clarification request from BSE regarding unusual price and volume movements in its shares. The company stated that there are no undisclosed events, information, or impending announcements that have a bearing on its operations, performance, or the price/volume behavior of its scrip. This indicates that any recent market activity is not attributable to internal, price-sensitive information from the company.

  • Company responded to BSE query on price movement.
  • No undisclosed events affecting operations or performance.
  • No information impacting share price or volume behavior.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SHILP GRAVURES LTD.-$ | Category: Clarification

item.title: [ISFL] ISF Exempt from SEBI Reg 24A due to capital & net worth limits
item.description:
ISF Ltd announced that Regulation 24A of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, requiring an Annual Secretarial Compliance Report, is not applicable to the company. This exemption stems from its paid-up equity share capital of ₹9.50 crore and net worth of ₹13.42 crore as of March 31, 2025. These figures are below the thresholds of ₹10 crore paid-up capital and ₹25 crore net worth specified in Regulation 15(2) for corporate governance provisions. The company confirmed it falls under clause (a) of Regulation 15(2) and committed to complying with the regulation within six months if its capital or net worth exceeds the specified limits in the future.

  • ISF Ltd is exempt from SEBI Regulation 24A compliance requirements.
  • Exemption is due to paid-up capital and net worth being below specified limits.
  • Paid-up capital is ₹9.50 crore; net worth is ₹13.42 crore as of March 2025.
  • The company will comply if it crosses the regulatory thresholds in future.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ISF LIMITED | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [SYMPHONY] Symphony's VAT Liability Reduced to ₹12,563 After Appeal
item.description:
Symphony Ltd received an order from the VAT Department, Lucknow, for FY 2016-17. The initial order, dated March 28, 2026, had incorrectly enhanced turnover and disallowed credit notes, leading to a tax liability of ₹13,70,40,625/-. Symphony filed an appeal, which was reviewed positively by the VAT authorities. Consequently, the total tax liability was significantly reduced to ₹12,563/-. The company will pay this amount within the prescribed time limit and stated that there is no material impact on its financials, operations, or other activities beyond this amount.

  • VAT liability for FY 2016-17 reduced from ₹13.7 Cr to ₹12,563.
  • Symphony's appeal against the initial VAT order was successful.
  • The company will pay the reduced amount within the prescribed time.
  • No material financial or operational impact is expected for Symphony.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Symphony Limited | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [IBULHSGFIN] Sammaan Capital CID Recommends Open Offer
item.description:
Sammaan Capital Ltd's Committee of Independent Directors (CID) has formally issued its written recommendation regarding the Open Offer, in compliance with Regulation 26(7) of the Takeover Regulations. This recommendation was published on April 10, 2026, in prominent newspapers including Financial Express, Jansatta, and Navshakti, mirroring the publication channels of the Detailed Public Statement. The company has also submitted copies of these newspaper publications to key regulatory bodies such as BSE Limited, National Stock Exchange of India Limited, and SEBI, along with Citigroup Global Markets India Private Limited, the manager to the Open Offer, fulfilling its regulatory obligations.

  • CID provided its written recommendation for the Open Offer.
  • Action taken in accordance with Takeover Regulation 26(7).
  • Recommendation published in major newspapers on April 10, 2026.
  • Copies submitted to BSE, NSE, SEBI, and the Offer Manager.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Indiabulls Housing Finance Ltd | Category: Open Offer

item.title: [APOLLOHOSP] Apollo Hospitals: NCLT directs shareholder meeting for scheme
item.description:
The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), Chennai Bench, has issued an order regarding a Composite Scheme of Arrangement involving Apollo Hospitals Enterprise Ltd (Demerged Company), Apollo Healthco Limited, Keimed Private Limited, and Apollo Healthtech Limited. The NCLT has directed Apollo Hospitals Enterprise Ltd to convene a meeting of its equity shareholders to consider the proposed scheme. Additionally, the Tribunal has dispensed with the requirement of convening meetings for certain preference shareholders and equity shareholders of the other applicant companies involved in the scheme. This order marks a significant procedural step in the corporate restructuring process for Apollo Hospitals and its associated entities.

  • NCLT Chennai directed Apollo Hospitals to convene an equity shareholder meeting.
  • The meeting will consider a Composite Scheme of Arrangement.
  • The scheme involves Apollo Hospitals, Apollo Healthco, Keimed, and Apollo Healthtech.
  • NCLT dispensed with meetings for other specific shareholder groups.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: APOLLO HOSPITALS ENTERPRISE LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [BANKBARODA] Bank of Baroda Annual Bond Disclosure as of March 31, 2026
item.description:
Bank of Baroda has submitted its annual disclosure for non-convertible securities as of March 31, 2026, in compliance with SEBI's operational circular dated May 22, 2024. The filing, presented in Annex - XIV-B, provides comprehensive details on the bank's listed bonds. It includes specific listing information for various ISINs, such as allotment and listing dates on BSE and NSE, along with the quantity issued. The disclosure also outlines record dates for upcoming interest payments and redemptions, some of which include call options, scheduled throughout 2025 and early 2026. Furthermore, it details the current credit ratings for these bonds, with most instruments receiving AAA/Stable ratings from agencies like CareEdge, India Rating, and CRISIL, while others hold AA+/Stable ratings from CRISIL and ICRA, all reaffirmed or affirmed as of early 2026.

  • Bank of Baroda disclosed annual bond details as of March 31, 2026, per SEBI regulations.
  • The filing lists various non-convertible securities with their listing information.
  • It specifies record dates for interest payments and redemptions through 2025-2026.
  • Most bonds hold AAA/Stable credit ratings from multiple agencies.
  • Some bonds are rated AA+/Stable by CRISIL and ICRA.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BANK OF BARODA | Category: Loan & Debt

item.title: [MARUTI] Maruti Suzuki Board to meet on April 28, 2026 for FY26 results & dividend
item.description:
Maruti Suzuki India Ltd has announced that its Board of Directors will convene a meeting on April 28, 2026. The primary agenda for this meeting is to consider and approve the audited financial results for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. Additionally, the board will discuss and potentially recommend a dividend on the company's equity shares for the financial year 2025-26. This intimation is made in compliance with Regulation 29 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Board meeting scheduled for April 28, 2026.
  • To approve audited financial results for FY26.
  • Board will consider recommending dividend for FY26.
  • Announcement made as per SEBI Listing Regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: MARUTI SUZUKI INDIA LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [ISFL] ISF Ltd not 'Large Corporate'; SEBI debt disclosure non-applicable
item.description:
ISF Ltd has clarified that it does not meet the criteria of a 'Large Corporate' (LC) as defined by SEBI Circular dated November 26, 2018. Consequently, the company is exempt from the requirements of filing initial and annual disclosures concerning fund raising through debt securities for the financial year 2025-26, as outlined in Annexures A, B1, and B2 of the said circular. This confirms the company's current operational scale relative to SEBI's LC classification.

  • ISF Ltd confirms non-applicability of SEBI 'Large Corporate' rules.
  • Exempt from debt securities disclosure for FY2025-26.
  • Avoids compliance burden under SEBI's large entity framework.
  • Indicates company's scale is below SEBI's LC threshold.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ISF LIMITED | Category: Clarification

item.title: [DENISCHEM] Denis Chem Lab clarifies share price movement is market-driven
item.description:
Denis Chem Lab responded to BSE's query regarding unusual share price movements. The company clarified that all material and price-sensitive information, as per SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, has been disclosed in a timely manner. They asserted that there is no pending information or announcement that could influence the stock's behavior. The company attributed the share price fluctuations solely to market conditions and stated that its management has no connection to these movements. They committed to continued timely disclosures.

  • Company clarified share price movement to BSE.
  • All material information disclosed timely per SEBI norms.
  • No pending information to impact stock price.
  • Price movement attributed purely to market conditions.
  • Management denies connection to price fluctuations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Denis Chem Lab Ltd | Category: Clarification
